degraded.reason: Small-complexity change, no diff-derived risk flags (is_io_boundary_change=False, is_cross_component_change=False): external LLM review (Advisory) and the full code-review subagent skipped per Override Classes. The classifier's 'large'/'touches_migrations' was a message-keyword false-positive (matched 'migration'/'migrate' in the prose); the diff touches no supabase/migrations and no cross-component machinery. 7-point self-review done; every change cited to file:line.
Pre-existing sweep-outbox warning from setup_iterate_worktree (trg-671bb0a2 status append dropped by a prior merge) — unrelated to this change; non-fatal (worktree still created).

surface_verification.justification: Pure orchestrator-internal Python change (run-config standalone-flag read locking); no web/cli/api surface and nothing UI-consumed. Verified by the shipwright-run unit suite (164 passed) incl. the cross-process concurrency integration test.

behaviors.behavior: load_run_config(migrate=False) returns the raw parsed config and performs NO legacy-migration write (no updated_at stamped on disk)
load_run_config(migrate=True) default still drops legacy pipeline entries (existing behavior preserved)
update_step's legacy-migration write now occurs INSIDE run_config_lock, never on the pre-lock standalone read
_read_standalone_flag mirrors _load_or_bootstrap(...).get('standalone', False) for absent / standalone-true / standalone-false configs
Existing multi-process run-config writers still never truncate or lose updates (regression guard for the WP2 atomic-write/lock invariant)
